description: Amiga boot block virus signatures -- the 14 most common boot sector viruses

signatures.name: SCA
signatures.description: First Amiga boot block virus (1987). Displays 'Something wonderful has happened' after 16th reboot. Hooks CoolCapture to survive warm reboots.

signatures.name: Byte Bandit

patterns.description: ASCII 'Virus by Byte Bndit' identification string

signatures.description: Byte Bandit (1988). Stealth boot block virus that intercepts trackdisk.device calls to hide from reading. Contains encrypted payload.

signatures.name: Lamer Exterminator

patterns.description: MOVEM.L D0-D7/A0-A6,-(SP); MOVEA.L $0004.W,A6 -- common decrypt routine entry
JSR _LVODoIO(A6) -- direct trackdisk DoIO call for block writes

signatures.description: Lamer Exterminator (1989). Destructive virus that slowly corrupts disk blocks. Multiple variants exist (1-4). Uses DoIO to write random data to disk blocks.

signatures.name: SADDAM
signatures.description: SADDAM virus (1991). Named after Saddam Hussein, infects boot blocks and displays political message. Multiple variants (A/B).

signatures.name: Byte Warrior
signatures.description: Byte Warrior (1988). Boot block virus by the Byte Warriors group. Hooks DoIO to intercept disk reads and infect clean disks.

signatures.name: North Star

patterns.description: MOVE.L D0,$0000002E -- CoolCapture vector hook for warm-reboot survival

signatures.description: North Star virus (1989). Hooks CoolCapture vector at $2E to survive warm reboots. Infects boot blocks of inserted disks via trackdisk.device.

signatures.name: Pentagon Circle

patterns.description: JSR _LVODoIO(A6); MOVEA.L -- DoIO hook pattern for disk interception

signatures.description: Pentagon Circle virus. Hooks DoIO to intercept disk reads and writes. Displays a star pattern on screen after infection count threshold.

signatures.name: Revenge
signatures.description: Revenge boot block virus. Infects boot sectors and hooks system vectors to persist across reboots.

signatures.name: Obelisk
signatures.description: Obelisk boot block virus. Displays a graphical obelisk pattern after a set number of reboots.

signatures.name: SystemZ
signatures.description: SystemZ boot block virus. Hooks CoolCapture and KickTag vectors for persistence. Infects disks inserted after boot.

signatures.name: CCCP

patterns.description: MOVE.L D0,$000000AE -- KickTag vector modification for persistence

signatures.description: CCCP boot block virus. Named after the Soviet Union. Modifies KickTag vector for cold-reboot persistence.

signatures.name: Graffiti
signatures.description: Graffiti / 16BitCrew boot block virus. Replaces boot block with custom code and displays graffiti-style message.

signatures.name: Disk-Doktor
signatures.description: Disk-Doktor boot block virus. Pretends to be a disk repair utility while corrupting file system structures. Destructive payload targets root block.

signatures.name: Australian Parasite
signatures.description: Australian Parasite boot block virus. One of several Australian-origin boot viruses. Hooks trackdisk.device for disk infection.
